meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM640 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM640 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The OM640 is Mercedes' compact transverse four-cylinder diesel — found in A‑Class and B‑Class models; reliable overall, but injector and timing chain issues are well documented. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 4 (A‑Class W169, B‑Class W245, A‑Class W176, B‑Class W246)
Confidence: 85% confidence — based on owner forums, specialist reports, and documented TSBs; proprietary enquiry figures are estimated.

rows.metric: Most repairs straightforward; injector work requires specialist equipment

rows.value: Widely available from Mercedes and aftermarket; OM640-specific parts well-stocked

rows.metric: Depends on host vehicle value — see R-to-V flag guidance

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The OM640 is a reasonably durable small diesel — but injector failure and timing chain stretch are known weaknesses — buy with full service history and proof of injector maintenance.
rows.value: Best For: Urban drivers, high-mileage commuters, and anyone seeking an economical diesel A‑Class or B‑Class.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM640 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED]. Transverse engine layout requires careful access.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.

cta.label: → Compare OM640 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an OM640 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned OM640 typically costs £1,800–£3,200 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement (1.5L, 1.7L or 2.0L). Fitting adds £550–£1,100 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the OM640 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The OM640 is considered reasonably durable, earning 3.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED] — some owners report 200,000+ miles, but injector failure is a known weakness. Regular servicing with correct oil specification is essential.

items.question: What is the most common OM640 failure?
items.answer: Injector failure is the most common issue — leaking injector seals can cause diesel contamination in the oil, leading to engine damage. Turbocharger failure and timing chain stretch are also reported on higher‑mileage examples.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the OM640?
items.answer: The OM640 powered the W169 A‑Class (2004–2012) and W245 B‑Class (2005–2011) in 1.5L and 2.0L variants, and later the W176 A‑Class (2012–2018) and W246 B‑Class (2011–2018) in 1.5L and 1.8L form.

items.question: How long does the OM640 last?
items.answer: With regular maintenance and early attention to injector issues, 150,000–200,000 miles is achievable. High‑mileage examples often require replacement of injectors and turbochargers.
